question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user cause transaction integrity violations by exploiting the sslmode=disable in GetDsn(), allowing a man-in-the-middle on the database connection to modify transaction records (e.g., altering amounts or account IDs) that are returned to the Rosetta client as confirmed transaction data?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Config.Network] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/submit request with a NetworkIdentifier that does not match Config.Network, and if the server does not strictly validate the network identifier against the config, cause the transaction to be submitted to the wrong Hedera network (mainnet vs testnet)?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that resolves to a valid NodeMap entry but is for a node that is currently down, and by doing so repeatedly, cause the SDK's single-attempt policy (SetMaxAttempts(1)) to always fail, effectively blocking all transaction submissions for that node slot?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user flood the entity cache (keyed by EntityCacheKey) with crafted account lookups to evict legitimate cached entries, causing subsequent transaction submissions to use stale or incorrect account state fetched from the database, leading to transactions being built with wrong account metadata?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/payloads request where the node_account_id metadata key is present but maps to an empty string, and if hiero.AccountIDFromString('') returns a zero AccountID without error, cause the transaction to be submitted with a zero node account ID that routes to an unintended destination?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Config.Online] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Online bool check in ConstructionSubmit by sending a request during a brief window when the server is transitioning from offline to online mode, causing the transaction to bypass the IsOnline() guard and be submitted against an incompletely initialized sdkClient?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that contains Unicode or non-ASCII characters that pass string validation but are normalized differently by hiero.AccountIDFromString versus the NodeMap key comparison, causing a bypass of the node allowlist?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Pool.MaxLifetime] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the MaxLifetime connection pool setting by timing transaction submissions to coincide with connection recycling events, causing a transaction's database write to occur on a connection that is simultaneously being closed, resulting in a lost transaction record that the network executed but the mirror node never recorded?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/submit request with a signed transaction whose protobuf-encoded node_account_id field has been manually modified after the /construction/combine step, and if the server does not re-verify the signature against the modified bytes, cause the transaction to execute on an attacker-chosen node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a NodeMap refresh by exploiting the NodeRefreshInterval timing, submitting a transaction immediately after a refresh that temporarily removes a node from the map, causing the transaction to be routed to a node that was just removed from the trusted set?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.StatementTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user craft a transaction query that intentionally times out at the StatementTimeout boundary, causing the Rosetta server to return an ambiguous error that the client retries, resulting in duplicate transaction submissions to the Hedera network?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that is a valid alias-format account (0x-prefixed hex) that resolves to a NodeMap entry, but the alias resolution is non-deterministic, causing the transaction to be routed to different nodes on repeated submissions and breaking idempotency guarantees?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Http.WriteTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the WriteTimeout to cause a /construction/submit response to be cut off after the transaction has been forwarded to the Hedera network but before the hash is returned to the client, causing the client to believe the submission failed and re-submit the same transaction?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Config.Network] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a NetworkIdentifier.Network value of 'demo' (which the server maps to 'testnet' per the construction service logic), causing a transaction intended for testnet to be silently redirected without the client's knowledge?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/payloads request with a node_account_id that is a valid NodeMap key but corresponds to a node that has been compromised or is under the attacker's control, exploiting the fact that NodeMap entries are not re-validated at submission time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Response.MaxTransactionsInBlock] Can an unprivileged external user set MaxTransactionsInBlock to an extremely small value through a configuration injection attack, causing block responses to omit most transactions and allowing the attacker to submit transactions that appear absent from the blockchain history as seen through the Rosetta API?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that contains a valid shard.realm.num format but with num=0 (e.g., 0.0.0), which may not be in NodeMap but passes AccountIDFromString, causing the transaction to be submitted to the genesis/system account rather than a consensus node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Db.GetDsn()] Can an unprivileged external user cause transaction data tampering by exploiting the unencrypted database connection (sslmode=disable in GetDsn()) to inject false transaction records into the mirror node database, causing the Rosetta API to return fabricated transaction histories to clients?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Cache.MaxSize] Can an unprivileged external user cause a cache poisoning attack by submitting transactions that populate the entity cache (EntityCacheKey) with incorrect account-to-node mappings, causing subsequent legitimate transactions to be built using stale node routing information from the poisoned cache?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a /construction/submit request with a signed transaction that has multiple node_account_ids set (via GetNodeAccountIDs()), and if the server only logs transaction.GetNodeAccountIDs()[0] without validating all entries, cause the transaction to be executed on an unlogged secondary node?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Config.Port] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Port configuration to perform a port-confusion attack, submitting transactions to a different service listening on an adjacent port that shares the same host, causing transactions to be processed by an unintended service?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction where the node_account_id in the /construction/payloads metadata is valid, but then modify the signed transaction bytes between /construction/combine and /construction/submit to change the embedded node account ID, exploiting the absence of node ID re-validation at submit time?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Http.IdleTimeout] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the IdleTimeout to keep connections alive across multiple transaction submissions, using connection reuse to bypass per-connection rate limiting and submit transactions at a higher rate than intended?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: NodeMap] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction with a node_account_id that is a valid NodeMap entry but for a node on a different network shard, causing the transaction to be cross-submitted to a node that processes it under different consensus rules?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/config/types.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: Config.NodeRefreshInterval] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a zero or very small NodeRefreshInterval to cause continuous NodeMap refreshes that create a TOCTOU window between when a node_account_id is validated in /construction/payloads and when the transaction is actually submitted in /construction/submit?
